2605.05197.title: Implicit Representations of Grammaticality in Language Models
2605.05197.paper_summary: Grammaticality and likelihood are distinct notions in human language. Pretrained language models (LMs), which are probabilistic models of language fitted to maximize corpus likelihood, generate grammatically well-formed text and discriminate well between grammatical and ungrammatical sentences in tightly controlled minimal pairs. However, their string probabilities do not sharply discriminate between grammatical and ungrammatical sentences overall. But do LMs implicitly acquire a grammaticality distinction distinct from string probability? We explore this question through studying internal representations of LMs, by training a linear probe on a dataset of grammatical and (synthetic) ungrammatical sentences obtained by applying perturbations to a naturalistic text corpus. We find that this simple grammaticality probe generalizes to human-curated grammaticality judgment benchmarks and outperforms LM probability-based grammaticality judgments. When applied to semantic plausibility benchmarks, in which both members of a minimal pair are grammatical and differ in only plausibility, the probe however performs worse than string probability. The English-trained probe also exhibits nontrivial cross-lingual generalization, outperforming string probabilities on grammaticality benchmarks in numerous other languages. Additionally, probe scores correlate only weakly with string probabilities. These results collectively suggest that LMs acquire to some extent an implicit grammaticality distinction within their hidden layers.
2605.05197.paper_summary_zh: 語法性和可能性是人類語言中不同的概念。預訓練的語言模型（LMs）是語言的概率模型，旨在最大化語料庫的可能性，生成語法上良好結構的文本，並能在嚴格控制的最小對中良好區分語法和非語法句子。然而，它們的字符串概率並未在整體上明確區分語法和非語法句子。但語言模型是否隱性地獲得了一種與字符串概率不同的語法性區分？我們通過研究語言模型的內部表示來探討這一問題，通過在一個由自然語言文本語料庫應用擾動獲得的語法和（合成）非語法句子數據集上訓練線性探針。我們發現這個簡單的語法性探針能夠推廣到人類策劃的語法性判斷基準，並且在表現上超越了基於語言模型概率的語法性判斷。當應用於語義合理性基準時，其中最小對的兩個成員都是語法的，且僅在合理性上有所不同，該探針的表現卻不如字符串概率。英語訓練的探針還顯示出非平凡的跨語言泛化，在許多其他語言的語法性基準中超越了字符串概率。此外，探針分數與字符串概率的相關性僅為微弱。這些結果共同表明，語言模型在其隱藏層中在某種程度上獲得了隱性的語法性區分。
2605.05197.author: Yingshan Susan Wang et.al.
2605.05197.authors: Yingshan Susan Wang, Linlu Qiu, Zhaofeng Wu, Roger P. Levy, Yoon Kim
